title: Filter tickets by priority and type (beads.rs)
description:
Implement ticket filtering (TRD 6.2):
- Filter by priority: single (P0) or multiple (P0,P1)
- Filter by type: single (TASK) or multiple (TASK,BUG)  
- Filters combine with AND logic
- No filters = all ready tickets pass through

Unit tests for all filter combinations.

title: AI editor prompt construction and launch (agent.rs)
description:
Implement AI agent launching (TRD 7.2):
- Construct prompt from ticket title and description: '<title>: <description>. Do not commit.'
- Launch Claude Code: claude --dangerously-skip-permissions '<prompt>'
- The command is launched inside the tmux window for each worktree
- Error if the AI editor binary is not found
- Command and args are configurable (from config.rs) for future editor swapping

title: Parse bd ready output (beads.rs)
description:
Implement beads ticket discovery (TRD 6.1):
- Run bd ready as a subprocess and capture output
- Parse output to extract ticket ID, title, description, priority, and type
- Define a Ticket struct with these fields
- Handle bd CLI not found with a clear error message
- Handle empty output (no ready tickets) gracefully

Unit tests with sample beads output.
close_reason: Implemented beads.rs with Priority, TicketType, Ticket types, ready() and parse_line() functions, plus 8 unit tests — all passing

title: Define error types (error.rs)
description:
Create error types using thiserror for all error conditions from TRD section 11:
- Not inside a tmux session
- No ready tickets after filtering
- bd CLI not found
- git worktree add failure
- AI editor binary not found
- Config file parse errors
- General I/O errors
close_reason: Defined Error enum with thiserror covering all TRD section 11 error conditions

title: List and remove worktrees (worktree.rs)
description:
Implement worktree listing and cleanup (TRD 6.4):
- List existing qld worktrees (for status command)
- Remove worktrees: git worktree remove <path> && git branch -d <branch>
- Support removing all qld worktrees or a specific one by ticket ID
- queensland clean — remove all qld worktrees
- queensland clean <ticket-id> — remove a specific worktree

Integration tests with real git repos.

title: Detect tmux session and create named windows (tmux.rs)
description:
Implement tmux integration (TRD 7.1):
- Detect if running inside a tmux session; error with 'queensland must be run inside a tmux session' if not
- Create new tmux windows named after the ticket ID
- Set working directory to the worktree path
- Idempotent: check for existing windows by name before creating new ones (TRD 7.3)
- Skip silently if window already exists (log at verbose level)

title: Create git worktrees from tickets (worktree.rs)
description:
Implement worktree creation (TRD 6.3):
- Derive branch name: qld/<ticket-id> (configurable prefix)
- Run: git worktree add -b qld/<ticket-id> <worktree-dir>/<ticket-id>
- Worktree directory defaults to <repo>/.qld-worktrees/
- Skip tickets that already have a worktree (idempotent)
- Log skipped tickets at verbose level
- If git worktree add fails, log error, skip ticket, continue with others

Integration tests with real git repos using tempfile.

title: M1: Beads Integration
description: Parse bd ready output, filter tickets by priority and type, with unit tests. This is the foundation milestone — all other features depend on having ticket data.

title: Implement --dry-run mode
description:
Add --dry-run flag to the run command (TRD 8):
- Show what would happen without doing it
- Print which tickets would get worktrees
- Print which tmux windows would be created
- Print the agent commands that would be launched
- No side effects (no git, no tmux, no subprocess calls)

title: Set up Cargo project with dependencies
description:
Initialize Cargo.toml with all dependencies from TRD section 12:
- clap 4 (derive feature)
- serde 1 (derive feature)
- toml 0.8
- thiserror 2
- tracing 0.1
- tracing-subscriber 0.3
Dev dependencies: tempfile 3, assert_cmd 2.

Create the project structure from TRD section 10:
src/main.rs, beads.rs, worktree.rs, tmux.rs, agent.rs, config.rs, error.rs
close_reason: Cargo deps, module stubs, and integration test dir created

title: CLI with clap: run, clean, status commands (main.rs)
description:
Implement CLI interface (TRD 8):
Commands:
- run: create worktrees and launch AI agents in tmux tabs
- clean: remove worktrees and their branches  
- status: show current worktrees and their ticket associations

Run options: --priority, --type, --dry-run, --worktree-only
Clean options: optional <ticket-id>
Global options: --config, --verbose, --quiet

Wire up all modules: beads → filter → worktree → tmux → agent.
Set up tracing-subscriber for logging with --verbose/--quiet control.

title: M4: CLI + Config
description: Clap CLI with run/clean/status commands, TOML config loading, --dry-run mode, end-to-end manual testing.

title: M2: Worktree Management
description: Create worktrees from ticket list, list and remove worktrees, idempotent creation (skip existing). Integration tests with real git.

title: M3: Tmux + Agent Launch
description: Detect tmux session, create named windows with correct working directory, launch Claude Code with ticket prompt, idempotent window creation.

title: TOML config loading (config.rs)
description:
Implement config loading (TRD 9):
- Load queensland.toml from project root (or --config path)
- Parse config structure:
  [queensland]
  worktree_dir = '.qld-worktrees'
  branch_prefix = 'qld'
  
  [agent]
  command = 'claude'
  args = ['--dangerously-skip-permissions']
  no_commit_instruction = 'Do not commit.'
- Provide sensible defaults when config file is missing
- Use serde + toml crate for deserialization

title: Implement status command
description:
Implement the status command (TRD 8):
- Show current qld worktrees and their associated ticket IDs
- Show branch names and worktree paths
- Optionally show if corresponding tmux windows are open
